top_videos.title: THE ORDINARY HAIR DENSITY SERUM | Dermatologist Review

top_objections.text: I was using 2% minoxidil for hair loss and I started getting headaches and dizzy and found they were side effects of people who were over 65 and since I’m 81 so I stopped. Sue me because I’d still like to look good 😂.

I'm a male with male pattern hair loss and thinning. I stopped using liquid minoxidil because of the irritation and flaky scalp which i never had before. I've been using this serum for just a couple of days and already noticing less to no shedding. i definitely use more than a couple of drops to get proper coverage and rub/massage it thoroughly which i feel like not everyone is doing.
Thank you for going over the ingredients. I really appreciate it since now we know how important those ingredients are and their side effects 😊

I have several autoimmune conditions. So when my hair started falling out again, after it had come back for the past year, I thought it was just a side effect I had to live with. 
Then I remembered I hadn't taken any collagen and zinc after my last bottle was finished 4 months ago.
Two DAYS of taking it and my hair loss has stopped. Im glad it stopped but could have saved like 25% of my hair. 😏

If you don't mind a little more body hair. As a male I welcomed it. Low dose Oral minoxidil has the same benefits without the scalp irritation. Online companies like strut gives a prescription that they fill too. 
The topical options do leave the hair and scalp a little dry and crispy. 
The combo of internal and external is the key. 
Also something like herbal saw palmetto that blocks dht could also address hair loss at it's cause. Good luck.
Try minoxidil in foam form instead. I had the same issue as you with the liquid form and my dermatologist recommended foam because people see less irritation with it
How long does a bottle last?
my dermatologists got me on cystine b6 and biotin and I've seen improvement when it comes to hair loss. I dont know if it's a gimmick since there are not many videos on these vitamins for hair loss.
I would not use in lash line, could be very irritating
You didn't mention Capyxl which is safe alternative to the menacing Minoxidil !!
I tried it for a month and didn’t do sh*t, it’s like putting water on my hair. Like literally nothing. What a waste of money
I know from my doctor and  I'm using it, it's very good, and also it doesn't have the side effects of Minoxidil."​ @Fools-Date-Nurses
Do you think it is safe with Minoxidil? I am using minoxidil and I wonder how I can adapt this product to my routine at the same time

top_positive_signals.text: I've used it 3-4 times a week, in conjunction with a 5 minute scalp massage every day, for about 4 months and my hair is definitely noticeable more dense (mom grabbed my braid and said "Oh my God, your hair has never been this thick!"), and the baby hairs that frame my face are thicker (actually larger hair strands, instead of fine baby wisps), longer, and more plentiful than they've ever been. I'm pleased enough with the results that I bought a 2nd bottle. It's not a quick fix, and you have to use it consistently for several months, but I am happy with it.
I had one bolding spot and started using this, only on that spot, maybe for two months and I think 3 times a week. Worked 100%, bold spot is gone!
I got this to thicken my hair but it actually cured my seb derm too, crazy!! Nothing has EVER worked for my seb derm, no shampoos nothing. So great!
Bless her! She’s doing the Lord’s work
I used it on hairline and got excellent results, regrowth of baby hairs was dramatic. My daughter in law uses it on her clients after  micro-needling. She claims to get excellent results.

I started having amla juice by mixing it with juice from aloe vera twice a day. Regularly trimming the hair also helps. Take care of your lifestyle. Use liitleextraa cocoonnoin organic hair oil shampoo as both are good for hair fall control. If you will do this every day, you will see results in a few weeks time.
I’ve been using this on my eyebrows for the past 2 months. And wow, they’re so much more full now. Just started using on my scalp. We’ll see. I have high hopes and little doubt about the results

I’m a 26 year old woman and have been using this for 2 years now. I swear by this product! I don’t have any hair loss disorders, but have always had very thin, fine hair, especially around my hairline. I originally began using this only on my hairline. I didn’t like leaving it in since it did make my hair greasy, so I put it in a few hours before I showered. I have noticed a huge increase in my hair thickness and density. My baby hairs have grown like crazy to even my hairline, I now look like I have full bangs as it’s growing out. I now put it all through my hair before showering and my hair looks so think and full. It even feels heavy when I wash it. It doesn’t make my hair grow faster, but definitely fuller and a little bit darker too (I’m blonde). I use it about once a week. Definitely my holy grail which I recommend to everyone now 💛

i noticed less hair fall after a continuous three month period of using littleexrtra reactiavate hair serum. I apply it every night and rinse it off with plain water in the morning. I also use cocooonion oil and shampoo once a week and apply an avocado mask every 15 days. Following this routine has really helped, and I'm seeing new hair growth in several areas of my head
I alternate this with rosemary oil each night. You don’t need much at all— I feel like those who feel greasy are using too much. As someone with fine, post menopausal hair, this works for me.

top_questions.text: Can we also as a society simply be ok with hair loss? I have androgenetic alopecia for decades now; I’ve tried almost everything out there, I wear a topper but on most days I just want to leave the house without a care in the world. Is that too much to ask? Please let’s normalize and accept hair loss in men and women. The pressure is just too much - mentally, physically and financially exhausting 😢

I had a coworker ( male) mention my beginning hair loss years ago. He thought it was a funny comment. It hurt. I can no longer go out without a wig for fear of someone staring or embarrassing me again. Society may not care but they do notice and at times call attention to it. Very painful. Vicki
​ @kehammer100 Wait - WHAT???? crud. I'm going to have to go find that video - I thought rosemary oil was good😢
I’m gonna start using this. Like this comment in a few months for an update❤
you dont know how much hope you spreaded with this, your contribution appreciated
Not sure if your review holds any credence as you don't even know how to spell "balding".....

I’m okay with your hair loss. I am not okay with mine. 
Why can’t people be okay and not be okay with whatever they choose? Common doesn’t equal normal. We should be figuring out WHY this seems to be occurring more and more.
Did you do the 5 minute scalp massages prior to that or did u start doing them at the same time as the serum?
She has given timestamps in the description. Or you could just scroll to the end. You had enough time to write a snarky comment, surely you’re not that busy.
I’m interested to hear what rosemary oil does please as I’m going thru menopause and after hot flushes notice my hair sheds. How Does rosemary oil help? Thank you
